2606.05602.title: Fix the Mind, Not the Move: Interpretable AI Assistance via Knowledge-Gap Localization
2606.05602.paper_summary: AI assistants in human-AI collaboration often correct suboptimal human actions through behavioral feedback (e.g., alerts or steering-wheel nudges in assistive driving). Such interventions can mitigate immediate errors, but long-term improvement requires addressing the underlying misconceptions that cause repeated mistakes. We introduce SENSEI, a framework that infers user misconceptions from interaction behavior and provides targeted, minimal yet sufficient suggestions to correct them. Our approach departs from action- or trajectory-level interventions by operating over a structured knowledge representation to localize and correct the sources of erroneous behavior. Across three long-horizon tasks with diverse misconceptions and corresponding behaviors, SENSEI demonstrates zero-shot compositional generalization, disentangling multiple overlapping misconceptions despite training only on single-misconception cases. A user study further shows that our method identifies real human misconceptions and provides effective guidance that improves long-horizon task performance, successfully correcting $90\%$ of student misconceptions. Code and project page are available at https://misoshiruseijin.github.io/SENSEI/.
2606.05602.paper_summary_zh: AI 助手在人類與 AI 的合作中，經常通過行為反饋（例如，在輔助駕駛中發出警報或輕推方向盤）來修正次優的人類行為。這些干預措施可以減少即時錯誤，但長期改善需要解決導致重複錯誤的根本誤解。我們介紹了 SENSEI，一個從互動行為中推斷用戶誤解的框架，並提供針對性的、最小但足夠的建議來修正它們。我們的方法不同於基於行動或軌跡的干預，通過在結構化知識表示上運作，以定位和修正錯誤行為的來源。在三個具有多樣化誤解和相應行為的長期任務中，SENSEI 展示了零樣本組合泛化，儘管僅在單一誤解案例上進行訓練，卻能解開多個重疊的誤解。一項用戶研究進一步顯示，我們的方法能識別真實的人類誤解，並提供有效的指導，改善長期任務的表現，成功修正了 $90\%$ 的學生誤解。代碼和項目頁面可在 https://misoshiruseijin.github.io/SENSEI/ 獲得。
2606.05602.author: Ayano Hiranaka et.al.
2606.05602.authors: Ayano Hiranaka, Ya-Chuan Hsu, Stefanos Nikolaidis, Erdem Bıyık, Daniel Seita
